title: coupon — subprocessors
description: The Coupon module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Stackra platform. Downstream marketing module engages ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) to receive marketing events fired from coupons with issuance_source IN ('referral', 'marketing_campaign'); the notifications module engages email/SMS subprocessors to deliver coupon lifecycle notifications; the tax module (finance::tax) engages tax-computation subprocessors (TaxJar / Avalara / Stripe Tax) — coupons feed into their calculation but do not directly engage. All are declared in their respective modules' subprocessors.json.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Forwarding Referral Coupon Redeemed + Campaign Coupon Redeemed + reversal events to ad networks (Meta CAPI, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, TikTok Events API, Microsoft UET, Snapchat CAPI) when coupon.issuance_source IN ('referral', 'marketing_campaign').
Delivering CouponUsageCapReachedNotification + CouponExpiredNotification + CouponRedeemedNotification (opt-in) + CouponClawbackNotification via email / SMS / in-app / push.

The tax module engages TaxJar / Avalara / Stripe Tax when computing tax on the post-discount invoice line. Coupons feed into that calculation via final_amount_cents but do not directly engage the tax providers.

Processing the actual invoice line + payment via Stripe / Paddle / Adyen. Coupon redemption is a service call inside the invoice-creation transaction; the invoice module dispatches the payment through its subprocessors.

None — referrals module also engages zero direct subprocessors. Coupons is a downstream of referrals (referrals issues coupons when a discount-reward vests); the reverse dependency (referrals module invoking a coupon subprocessor) does not exist.

notes: This file exists deliberately with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

The Wave 4 tenant-facing 'subprocessor disclosure' page reads THIS FILE'S downstream_subprocessors_referenced list to compose the full disclosure — every downstream that this module transitively engages is surfaced to the tenant admin.

Ad-network subprocessors (Meta / Google / TikTok / Microsoft / Snapchat) are the highest-risk category — they receive PII (hashed) + click IDs + conversion values (source_amount_cents). Tenants opt into which providers via marketing module's provider configuration.
